summary: Add a comment to internal/mcp/cobratree/shellout.go explaining the intent of the raw args passthrough.
reason: Greptile flagged the raw `args` passthrough as a potential CLI-flag-injection vector. It's intentional (mirrors printing-press v4 design — lets MCP callers reach typed flags not in the tool schema) but worth documenting for security reviewers.
validated_outcome: Comment in place describing the design (no shell, exec.CommandContext executes argv directly) and the trust boundary (hosts run this MCP behind their own access control).
